title: [InstCombine] Fix unsafe PHINode cast and simplify logic in PointerReplacer
summary: The developers generalized the optimization by decoupling the availability check from the instruction type validation in PHI node handling. Originally, the code used a single lambda that both verified incoming values were instructions and checked their availability, but this prematurely set `IsReplaceable` to false for non-instruction values (like poison/null). The fix splits this into two distinct checks: first confirming all incoming values are instructions, then verifying their availability. This allows the transformation to correctly handle poison and null pointers in PHI nodes, which were previously excluded. The key insight is separating validation steps to avoid early exclusion of valid patterns, enabling broader applicability while maintaining safety. This approach can be applied to other optimizations by isolating precondition checks from core logic to preserve optimization opportunities across edge cases.
